 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.19","title":"Optional: ligatures/shaping pipeline (Harfbuzz)","description":"Goal
- Support code-font ligatures and better complex-script shaping when it materially improves UX.

Acceptance Criteria
- Shaping is feature-gated and does not regress perf by default.
- Golden checks prove layout stability.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","title":"Implement: new crate frankenterm-web (wasm-bindgen API + build plumbing)","description":"Goal
- Add `crates/frankenterm-web` with WASM bindings and a minimal JS-facing API.

Requirements
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- Expose: init(canvas_id or handle), resize, feed_input, present_patch.

Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` succeeds.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.20","title":"Optional: auto URL detection (non-OSC8) + safe click handling","description":"Goal
- Detect plain-text URLs in the viewport/scrollback and make them clickable (similar to xterm.js WebLinks addon).

Scope
- Heuristics tuned to avoid false positives in code.
- Security: sanitize and enforce allowed protocols.

Testing
- Fixture corpus for common URL forms.
- E2E: click opens expected target (or emits link event) with JSONL logs.
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:16.284093147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:16.284093147Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.20","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:16.284093147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.20","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:16.284093147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.20","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:16.284093147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","title":"Implement: WebGPU renderer skeleton (device init + background cell quads)","description":"Goal
- Get pixels on screen: render the terminal background + cell backgrounds at interactive speeds.

Requirements
- WebGPU init (adapter/device/queue) + resize handling.
- Draw pass for background and per-cell bg colors.
- Path for applying dirty-span patches only (no full redraw required).

Acceptance Criteria
- A minimal web harness can render a synthetic grid and resize without artifacts.

+{"id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","title":"Implement: WebGPU renderer skeleton (device init + background cell quads)","description":"Goal
- Get pixels on screen: render the terminal background + cell backgrounds at interactive speeds.

Requirements
- WebGPU init (adapter/device/queue) + resize handling.
- Draw pass for background and per-cell bg colors.
- Path for applying dirty-span patches only (no full redraw required).

Acceptance Criteria
- A minimal web harness can render a synthetic grid and resize without artifacts.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","title":"Implement: glyph rasterization + atlas cache (monospace first)","description":"Goal
- Turn glyphs into atlas textures with stable metrics and minimal overhead.

Requirements
- Start with monospace ASCII + box drawing + common symbols; expand to full Unicode coverage via bd-lff4p.2.18.
- Deterministic glyph keys/ids across runs and platforms (within a given font build).
- Cache with explicit eviction policy + metrics (hit/miss, bytes, upload cost).
- Hot path: no allocations when glyph already cached.

Testing
- Unit tests for key stability and eviction invariants.
- Golden fixtures for a representative glyph set (metrics + atlas coordinates).
- Bench harness integration to measure cache miss cost and steady-state throughput.

Acceptance Criteria
- Benchable API: given a glyph key, returns atlas coords and advances with no allocations in hot path.
- Upload + packing strategy is measurable and regression-gated.
","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldForest","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:09:31.782856563Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","title":"Implement: WebGPU text rendering (atlas + instanced quads)","description":"Goal
- Render terminal text cells using the glyph atlas and an instanced quad pipeline.

Requirements
- Respect per-cell fg color + attrs (bold/italic/underline as feasible).
- Handle wide glyphs and combining marks (at least gracefully).

Acceptance Criteria
- 120x40 grid @ 60fps with typical demo workloads on modern desktop.
- Instrumented frame timing (CPU submit + GPU time if possible).
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","title":"Implement: cursor + selection + hyperlink hover/click rendering","description":"Goal
- Make the terminal feel interactive: cursor, selection visualization, and hyperlink affordances.

Requirements
- Cursor rendering styles: block, bar, underline.
- Selection rendering + copy.
- Hyperlink hover underline + click dispatch.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic visual tests (golden images or checksumable framebuffer dumps).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.1","title":"Implement: new crate ftui-web (WASM API surface + glue)","description":"Goal
- Add `crates/ftui-web` exposing a minimal embedding API for running ftui models in WASM.

API Sketch
- `WasmApp::new(opts)`
- `resize(cols, rows)`
- `input(event_bytes or semantic event)`
- `tick(dt)`
- `render() -> Patch or Frame`

Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check -p ftui-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` succeeds.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.10","title":"Docs: embedding ftui-web + frankenterm-web into frankentui_website","description":"Goal
- Provide exact integration instructions for the Next.js site (bun) to consume the wasm artifacts.

Acceptance Criteria
- Clear build steps, file locations, and runtime initialization.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.2","title":"Refactor: runtime backend abstraction (no direct Crossterm usage)","description":"Goal
- Introduce a backend abstraction so ftui-runtime can run on:
  - native tty backend (custom)
  - wasm backend (JS-driven)

Acceptance Criteria
- `Program` no longer owns a concrete `TerminalSession` tied to Crossterm.
- Backends provide: size, input events (or bytes), present output (or patches), and mode toggles.
- Old code paths removed after migration (no lingering shims).

- Ensure the ftui crates required for demos compile for `wasm32-unknown-unknown`.

Scope

- Ensure deterministic time sources can be injected.

Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check --workspace --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` succeeds for the relevant crate set.

","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreuseStream","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:10.884122826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:18:11.011217317Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:10.884122826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:10.884122826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1011,"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ChartreuseStream: ftui-extras and ftui umbrella now compile for wasm32. All core crates pass. Remaining: ftui-runtime needs cfg-gating of std::thread, mpsc, TerminalSession for wasm32 (overlaps bd-lff4p.4.6). Commit 4ca80e69.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:18:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","title":"Implement: WASM step-based Program runner (no blocking poll, no threads)","description":"Goal
- Implement a deterministic, JS-driven runtime loop for WASM.

Requirements
- Inputs are delivered from JS (keyboard/mouse/resize/ticks).
- Rendering produces either ftui-render Buffer diffs or FrankenTerm patches.
- No `std::thread` assumptions.

Acceptance Criteria
- Can drive an ftui Model with: init -> input -> view -> present cycles.
- Deterministic record/replay possible.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:17.338911499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:17.338911499Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:17.338911499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:17.338911499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:17.338911499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.5","title":"Integrate: ftui-render Buffer -> FrankenTerm patch feed","description":"Goal
- Connect ftui-render output to FrankenTerm so the browser renderer can present ftui frames efficiently.

Options
- Convert ftui-render Buffer diffs into FrankenTerm patch representation.
- Or define a shared patch protocol used by both.

Acceptance Criteria
- No full-frame copies on steady-state updates.
- End-to-end demo updates are visibly smooth.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:23.296518547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:23.296518547Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:23.296518547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:23.296518547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:23.296518547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:23.296518547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","title":"Demo: run ftui-demo-showcase in browser via ftui-web + FrankenTerm","description":"Goal
- Run `ftui-demo-showcase` fully client-side in the browser via `ftui-web` + `frankenterm-web` (no xterm.js).

Scope
- Boot, render, navigate, resize, and respond to keyboard + mouse with low latency.
- Any OS-dependent features must either:
  - have explicit web implementations, OR
  - be explicitly disabled with user-visible UX (no silent partial failure).
- Perf + stability: no leaks, no jank, and predictable frame-time under typical demo workloads.

Testing + Logging (Non-negotiable)
- Create a browser "demo sweep" analogous to `tests/e2e/scripts/test_demo_showcase_sweep.sh`:
  - emits detailed JSONL (screen_id, seed, size, DPR, timings, frame/patch hashes)
  - failures emit minimal repro traces that can be replayed
- Determinism: recorded sessions replay to identical hashes on the same build.

Acceptance Criteria
- A single web page can run the demo for 10+ minutes without runaway memory or degraded responsiveness.
- JSONL logs validate against the shared schema and are rich enough to debug mismatches.
- Seed/time-freeze determinism is preserved end-to-end.
